frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar every time women talk like all men are unsafe, it feels unfair also. Main toh aisa nahi hoon, and there are plenty of decent men who get judged for no reason just because some idiots do nonsense. Being careful is fine, but assuming the worst about every man is also not right.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering men's feelings of being unfairly judged. It encodes the 'not all men' response, self-exemption ('main toh aisa nahi hoon'), and frames women's wariness as irrational overgeneralization.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
